Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 09:33 ET
Index puts surge to 1.58 P/C while single-names favor calls at 0.87
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. Institutions buying index protection while maintaining single-name upside exposure — classic dispersion
  • 2. SPY negative gamma at -$5.2B amplifies moves into Core PCE Thursday
  • 3. TSLA leads call flow despite broad bearish sentiment at F&G 19
NARRATIVE — hedged dispersion positioning

The market exhibits a pronounced institutional hedging pattern with index P/C spiking to 1.58 while single-name P/C remains call-heavy at 0.87 — a 0.71 divergence indicating sophisticated dispersion positioning. Massive SPY put blocks dominated flow (625P: 125k, 640P: 124k, Apr 2 640P: 114k) even as TSLA call volume reached 113k contracts on the Mar 25 385C strike. This bifurcation occurs against a bearish regime backdrop with sentiment at -29 and Fear & Greed at 19, yet implied correlation remains moderate at 0.37, confirming the stock-picking environment that justifies this positioning structure. SPY's negative gamma environment at -$5.2B creates mechanical amplification risk ahead of Core PCE Thursday, with the gamma flip sitting just $8 above current levels at $667. The put wall at $640 provides downside support, but any catalyst-driven move below current levels triggers dealer selling that accelerates declines independent of fundamental views. Twenty names including AAPL, AMD, and AMZN show backwardation — elevated near-term fear priced into individual names even as the broader index hedge demand concentrates in March 27 strikes. Technical divergence adds complexity as XLE posts a 91.0 bullish score with RSI at 80 despite energy's historical correlation sensitivity, while MSFT shows extreme oversold conditions (RSI 28, score 12.9) after Millennium's aggressive Q4 build to $2.49B. The combination of institutional hedging, negative gamma amplification, and Thursday's PCE catalyst creates a regime where single-name opportunities exist within a structurally protected portfolio framework.

Layer 1: Sentiment -29Layer 2: SPY GEX -$5.2BLayer 3: P/C divergence 1.58/0.87Layer 4: IC 0.37Layer 5: XLE score 91.0
TSLABULLISH
Call dominance into volatility
TSLA 385C block of 113k contracts leads single-name call flow despite broad bearish sentiment at F&G 19. Stock shows backwardation with elevated near-term IV, creating asymmetric upside expression into earnings season setup.
L3: 385C 113k blockL4: backwardation regimeL1: contrarian to -29 sentiment
Long TSLA Mar 25 385C vs put spread financing
XLEBULLISH
Energy breakout confirmation
XLE posts strongest technical score (91.0) with golden cross and RSI overbought at 80, confirming sector rotation momentum. Energy vol shows rich IV/flat skew divergence, suggesting options market hasn't caught up to technical breakout.
L5: technical score 91.0L5: RSI overbought 80L4: vol/skew divergence
Long XLE calls, short vol via put spreads

ATM IV = at-the-money implied vol  |  IV PCTILE = where today's IV ranks vs ≈90 trading days (green=cheap, red=expensive)  |  Spread = ATM IV − RV 20d  |  Rich: IV/RV >1.2×  |  Skew 90/110: P(90%) − C(110%)  |  SKEW PCTILE: vs ≈90d history  |  PUT/CALL V%: daily volume rank vs ≈90d history  |  vs SECTOR: ticker ATM IV / sector ETF ATM IV (±10% threshold)  |  Divergence: cross-signal flags (VOL HIGH/SKEW FLAT, CHEAP VOL/RICH SKEW, etc.)
